asana.description: Asana MCP exposes the Asana REST API as structured tools. It can create and update projects and tasks, manage teams and workspaces, handle custom fields, tags, portfolios, goals, stories/comments, memberships, time tracking entries, and more. It is optimized for natural-language use: many tools default workspace and user from your current Asana session, and descriptions explain how to get each GID from other tools.
capabilities: Create, read, update, and delete Asana projects
Create, read, update, and delete tasks and subtasks
Associate tasks with projects, sections, tags, and user task lists
Search tasks in a workspace with rich filters
Manage followers on projects and tasks
Create and manage sections within projects
Create and manage portfolios and portfolio items
Create and manage goals, time periods, and supporting relationships
Create and manage custom fields and enum options
Create and manage tags in a workspace and attach/detach them from tasks
Create and manage memberships for projects, portfolios, goals, and custom fields
Create and manage teams and team memberships
Add/remove users to/from workspaces and teams
Create and read stories (comments) on tasks
Upload and manage attachments on tasks and projects
Create and read time tracking entries on tasks
Batch multiple Asana API calls via a single batch tool
Fetch events to monitor changes on tasks, projects, tags, etc.
not_supported: Allocations and resource management APIs (all allocation tools removed)
Task templates and goal templates APIs (GET_TASK_TEMPLATES removed)
Direct user custom field updates via UPDATE_USER (must use UPDATE_USER_FOR_WORKSPACE instead)
Any Asana feature not exposed by the public REST API or disabled by plan limits (for example, Resource Management without an Enterprise plan)
asana.setup_notes: Requires an Asana OAuth app (client ID, client secret, redirect URI) and user consent. Environment variables ASANA_CLIENT_ID, ASANA_CLIENT_SECRET, and ASANA_REDIRECT_URI must be set. Tokens are cached and refreshed automatically; no configuration of workspaces or users is needed inside the tools themselves.
asana.workflow: At runtime: authenticate once → use GET_CURRENT_USER to learn your user GID and workspaces → list or search projects/tasks with GET_MULTIPLE_PROJECTS / GET_MULTIPLE_TASKS / SEARCH_TASKS_IN_WORKSPACE → create or update entities (CREATE_A_PROJECT, CREATE_A_TASK, UPDATE_A_TASK, UPDATE_PROJECT) → link tasks to projects, sections, tags, and goals (ADD_PROJECT_FOR_TASK, ADD_TASK_TO_SECTION, ADD_TAG_TO_TASK, ADD_SUPPORTING_RELATIONSHIP) → manage memberships, followers, time tracking, and portfolios/goals as needed.
asana.file_handling: The Asana MCP does not manage local media files. Attachments are handled via Asana’s attachment endpoints by providing either a file path in the workspace (for uploads) or an external URL. All other tools operate purely on Asana resource IDs (GIDs) and JSON payloads.

tools_summary.note: All tools are defined in tools_manifest.json. This metadata file highlights common and frequently used tools; other tools follow the same patterns.

tools.name: GET_CURRENT_USER
tools.description: Returns the authenticated user's Asana record (gid, name, email, workspaces). Often the first call to establish context and get workspace GIDs.

tools.name: GET_MULTIPLE_WORKSPACES
tools.description: Lists all workspaces available to the authenticated user.

tools.name: CREATE_A_PROJECT
tools.description: Creates a new project in a workspace (and optionally a team).

parameters.workspace_gid: string (required) – Workspace GID from GET_CURRENT_USER or GET_MULTIPLE_WORKSPACES.
parameters.name: string (required) – Project name.
parameters.team_gid: string (optional) – Team GID when workspace is an organization.
parameters.default_view: string (optional) – list, board, calendar, or timeline.

tools.name: CREATE_A_TASK
tools.description: Creates a new Asana task; requires workspace, parent, or projects for association.

parameters.opt_fields: array<string> (optional) – Extra fields to include in response.

tools.name: UPDATE_A_TASK
tools.description: Updates attributes of an existing task (name, notes, assignee, dates, projects, etc.).

parameters.task_gid: string (required) – Task GID from GET_MULTIPLE_TASKS, GET_TASKS_FROM_A_PROJECT, or CREATE_A_TASK.
parameters.data: object (required) – Fields to update, e.g. {"name": "Updated name", "completed": true}.

tools.name: GET_MULTIPLE_TASKS
tools.description: Retrieves a list of tasks filtered by assignee, project, section, tag, user task list, or date ranges.

parameters.completed_since: string (optional) – Return tasks completed since this ISO date.
parameters.modified_since: string (optional) – Return tasks modified since this ISO date.

tools.name: ADD_TAG_TO_TASK
tools.description: Adds an existing tag to a task (tags cannot be added via UPDATE_A_TASK).

parameters.task_gid: string (required) – Task GID from GET_MULTIPLE_TASKS or GET_TASKS_FROM_A_PROJECT.
parameters.tag_gid: string (required) – Tag GID from GET_TAGS or CREATE_TAG.

tools.name: ADD_TASK_DEPENDENCIES
tools.description: Adds dependency relationships to a task, making other tasks prerequisites.

parameters.task_gid: string (required) – Task that depends on other tasks.
parameters.dependencies: array<string> (required) – Task GIDs that must be completed first.

tools.name: ADD_SUPPORTING_RELATIONSHIP
tools.description: Links a project, task, portfolio, or another goal as a supporting resource for a goal.

parameters.goal_gid: string (required) – Goal to add support to (from GET_GOALS or CREATE_GOAL).
parameters.data: object (required) – {"supporting_resource": "GID", "contribution_weight": 0|1}. supporting_resource from GET_MULTIPLE_PROJECTS, GET_MULTIPLE_TASKS, GET_PORTFOLIOS, or GET_GOALS.

tools.name: CREATE_TAG
tools.description: Creates a new tag in a workspace for categorizing tasks.

tools.name: CREATE_TIME_TRACKING_ENTRY
tools.description: Creates a time tracking entry on a task.

parameters.task_gid: string (required) – Task GID from GET_MULTIPLE_TASKS or GET_TASKS_FROM_A_PROJECT.
parameters.attributable_to: string (optional) – Project GID to attribute time to (for tasks in multiple projects).
parameters.approval_status: string (optional) – APPROVED, DRAFT, REJECTED, or SUBMITTED.

tools.name: SUBMIT_PARALLEL_REQUESTS
tools.description: Uses the Asana Batch API to execute multiple requests in parallel (up to 10 actions).

tools.name: GET_EVENTS
tools.description: Retrieves Asana events for a resource using sync tokens (two-step long-poll flow).

parameters.resource_gid: string (required) – GID of the resource to watch (task, project, tag, etc.).
parameters.sync: string (optional) – Sync token from a previous call (omit for initial request).
parameters.opt_fields: array<string> (optional) – Optional fields in event records.
